hero.title: 5 Days, The Golden Triangle.
hero.titleAccent: Delhi, the Taj Mahal and the Pink City.
hero.intro: The classic first trip to India, and for good reason. Old Delhi's chaos and spice, the Taj Mahal glowing at sunrise, and Jaipur's hilltop forts and bazaars. Three iconic cities, five unforgettable days.

breadcrumb.label: 5 Days Delhi Agra Jaipur + Taj Mahal (Golden Triangle)

hero.heroImageAlt: Taj Mahal Agra at sunrise Golden Triangle

overview.heading: 5 Days Delhi Agra Jaipur + Taj Mahal (Golden Triangle): The Full Picture
body: The Golden Triangle is the most popular route in all of India, linking the capital Delhi, the Taj Mahal in Agra and the royal Pink City of Jaipur. It is the ideal introduction for first-time visitors, packing in Mughal monuments, royal palaces and bustling bazaars across a comfortable five-day loop.

This 5 day Golden Triangle tour package from Malaysia is fully private, with your own air-conditioned vehicle and driver guide throughout. You see Old and New Delhi, witness the Taj Mahal at sunrise when it is at its most magical, and explore Jaipur's forts and markets, all without the constraints of a coach group.
It is the trip that turns first-timers into India lovers. More beautiful in person than any photograph, and easy enough that you spend your energy on the experiences rather than the logistics.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Taj Mahal at Sunrise
items.body: Seeing the Taj Mahal as the sun rises, the white marble shifting through pink and gold, is one of travel's great moments. We time your visit for the best light and the smaller crowds.

items.title: Old & New Delhi
items.body: From the spice-scented lanes of Chandni Chowk and the grand Jama Masjid to the colonial avenues and India Gate, Delhi is a city of dramatic contrasts and deep history.
items.imageAlt: Old Delhi Chandni Chowk and Jama Masjid

items.title: Amber Fort & Jaipur's Pink City
items.body: Jaipur delivers the romance: the hilltop Amber Fort, the City Palace, Hawa Mahal and bazaars overflowing with gems and textiles. Rajasthan at its most regal.

days.title: Arrive Delhi
days.description: Met at Indira Gandhi International Airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free to rest or explore nearby.

days.title: Delhi Sightseeing, Drive to Agra
days.description: A morning of Old and New Delhi, then drive to Agra in the afternoon.

days.title: Taj Mahal Sunrise, Agra Fort, Drive to Jaipur
days.description: Sunrise at the Taj Mahal, then Agra Fort, before driving to Jaipur via Fatehpur Sikri.

days.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
days.description: A full day exploring the Pink City's forts, palaces and bazaars.

days.title: Depart Jaipur
days.description: Checkout and transfer to Jaipur airport for your onward flight. Some itineraries return via Delhi on request.

whyBook.heading: The Golden Triangle, Perfected Over 35 Years
whyBook.body: We have run the Delhi Agra Jaipur route more times than we can count. We know the guides who bring the history alive, the hotels with the right rooms, and how to time the Taj at sunrise. Dhesu has sent Malaysians to India since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: Golden Triangle Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
The Golden Triangle is the definitive first India trip, and this 5 day version covers it at a comfortable pace. Delhi, Agra and Jaipur each bring something distinct: the capital's history, the Taj Mahal's beauty, and Jaipur's royal colour.

Going private makes a real difference here. Your own vehicle and guide mean you reach the Taj at sunrise, skip the coach-group waits, and shop the bazaars without a clock ticking.

Want to add more? Extend with Amritsar, Kashmir, Shimla or Manali in our longer Golden Triangle combination packages.

sections.heading: Best Time for the Golden Triangle
sections.body:
October to March is the prime window, with comfortable days for sightseeing and cool evenings. The northern plains get extremely hot from April to June, and the monsoon arrives July to September.

Winter mornings can be misty in Delhi and Agra, which occasionally affects Taj visibility, but it also brings the most atmospheric light. We plan your Taj timing to give the best chance of a clear, golden sunrise.

sections.heading: How to Extend the Golden Triangle
sections.body:
The Golden Triangle is a brilliant base to build on. Popular add-ons include Amritsar and the Golden Temple (7 days), Kashmir's lakes and meadows (8 to 9 days), and the hill stations of Shimla or Manali (9 to 10 days).

If the Taj and Rajasthan are your priority, the 7 day comfort version gives you two nights in each city for a more relaxed pace.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (DEL). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for the Golden Triangle?
items.answer: October to March is ideal, with pleasant days and cool nights. April to June is very hot. July to September is the monsoon. Winter mornings can be misty around the Taj, but generally offer the most atmospheric light.

items.question: Is the Taj Mahal visit at sunrise?
items.answer: Yes, we time your Taj Mahal visit for sunrise, when the marble glows and the crowds are smaller. The exact timing depends on the season and your hotel location, which your guide will arrange.

items.question: Can I add Amritsar, Kashmir or the hills?
items.answer: Yes. The Golden Triangle combines beautifully with Amritsar (7 days), Kashmir (8 to 9 days), Shimla (9 days) or Manali (10 days). We have dedicated packages for each combination.

items.question: Is this trip suitable for first-time India travellers?
items.answer: It is the ideal first India trip. The route is well established, the sights are iconic, and the private vehicle and guide make the logistics effortless so you can focus on the experience.
